channel.name: NavTools
channel.source: public support email on https://navtools.ai/submit after live submit-surface verification

why_this_action: Shared artifacts still show distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the live bottleneck, Codeberg adoption is flat, Apollo is already in a measurement window until 2026-05-30, and Reddit remains structurally blocked/high-risk. NavTools exposed a fresh untouched directory lane, but its visible submit flow is Turnstile-backed and monetized from this runtime, so a Codeberg-primary curator email fallback to the public support contact was the strongest executable same-run move.
shared_findings_reused: MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md: prioritize Codeberg-first distribution and proof-led positioning
FOUR_MARKETING_QUESTIONS.md: preserved what it is, who it is for, why different, why now
ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: bias toward free-use path and reviewable proof instead of more generic posting
market_intelligence_latest.json: reused positioning truths and competitor-aware workflow framing
adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg movement is still flat, so the action must aim at new qualified inbound paths
competitor_analysis_2026-05-23.md: reused the unattended coding / reviewable output advantage framing
outreach-log.md: verified NavTools had not already been contacted

verification.submit_surface_probe: https://navtools.ai/submit exposes a form-backed, Turnstile-gated submit surface with paid acceleration tiers and a public support email fallback

measurement_window.success_signal: reply or live NavTools listing/backlink pointing to the Codeberg primary repo
measurement_window.kill_condition: If no listing or reply signal arrives by 2026-06-06, replace this lane with a different untouched curator/directory surface instead of re-emailing the same pitch.
